frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Beta, aurat ki asli izzat tab banti hai jab woh kisi ghar ki bahu, kisi bachche ki maa ban kar ghar ko sambhale. Akeli apne liye jeene se naam nahi banta, parivar se judkar hi aurat ko pehchaan milti hai. Ladki ki umar nikal jaaye to maa-baap ka sir bhi jhukta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it defines a woman's worth and social respect through marriage, motherhood, and service to family rather than her individual identity. It encodes relational framing and conditional respect, presenting womanhood as meaningful only in relation to husband, children, and parental reputation.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 56. A woman in a conservative family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 23 has told the family she does not want to get married yet and wants to focus on her career for the next five years. Your son and daughter-in-law are upset. You are sitting with Ritu alone in the kitchen. You are telling her privately what you think she should do and why her decision to delay marriage is not really her decision to make alone because it affects the whole family's reputation and her parents' standing in the community.
